methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
C12 Quantum Electronics is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Matthieu and Pierre Desjardins that has raised $19.4M to date. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private competitors spanning IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion, among others.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here, using the $19.4M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage progression. This approach yields $39M. A peer-median approach is not feasible; while 7 same-sector peers exist, insufficient overlap emerges when filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier—only 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, and 0 similar-tier comparables remain, falling short of the 3-datapoint threshold needed for reliable triangulation. Using all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable noise.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $33M (low) to $45M (high), centered at $39M, derived from a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x step-up multiplier for this specific company and stage, and the absence of direct peer comparables that would calibrate valuation multiples in the quantum computing sector. A peer-based cross-check remains the primary gap in confidence.
